tl_dr: DDIM-GMM adds moment-matched Gaussian-mixture DDIM kernels and reports improved few-step class-conditional ImageNet-256 sampling.

reasoning_brief: The abstract and Tables 2/5/10 report ImageNet-1K 256 FID50K under flow-map initialized and from-scratch settings.
risks_or_unknowns: Flow-matching-initialized table rows are partially ambiguous in PDF text extraction.
title: Distribution Matching Distillation without Fake Score Network
tl_dr: FSF-DMD removes the auxiliary fake-score network from DMD-style flow-map distillation and reports ImageNet-1K 256 two-step results.

reasoning_brief: The abstract and Tables 1-2 report ImageNet 256x256 gFID with and without classifier-free guidance.
title: FrequencyBooster: Full-Frequency Modeling for High-Fidelity Pixel Diffusion
tl_dr: FrequencyBooster adds a high-capacity full-frequency decoder to pixel diffusion and reaches 1.60 gFID on ImageNet-256.

reasoning_brief: The abstract and Table 5 report ImageNet-256 FID-50K/IS for FLUX.2, VA-VAE, and REPA-E tokenizers. Rows are controlled 80-epoch comparisons rather than SOTA-scale training.
title: Aligning Latent Geometry for Spherical Flow Matching in Image Generation
tl_dr: Spherical-slerp latent flow matching aligns VAE latent support geometry and improves matched ImageNet-256 FID across tokenizers and SiT scales.

reasoning_brief: The abstract and Tables 2/5 report ImageNet-1K 256 FID for SSC at NFE=50. Some guided/REPA rows are visually clear but text extraction merges labels.
risks_or_unknowns: Guided and REPA table rows need visual cleanup before adding more CSV rows.
title: The Velocity Deficit: Initial Energy Injection for Flow Matching
tl_dr: Velocity Deficit identifies integration lag in flow matching and proposes a training-free scale schedule corrector for faster ImageNet-256 sampling.

reasoning_brief: The paper is conceptually relevant to flow matching but does not report ImageNet-256 generation metrics.
risks_or_unknowns: No leaderboard row because experiments use feature-space MMD rather than image generation FID.
title: Support-Conditioned Flow Matching Is Kernel Smoothing
tl_dr: Support-conditioned flow matching is shown to reduce to kernel smoothing under Gaussian OT paths, with experiments on DINOv2 ImageNet features.
